meta.title: SAT Math 800 Prep: Live Classes for the Digital SAT
meta.description: Live online SAT Math prep for the Digital SAT: Heart of Algebra, Advanced Math, Data Analysis, Desmos strategy and full adaptive mock cycles with a mentor.
keywords: sat math classes for high school students
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + weekly timed practice
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + documented mock-score progression

program_overview.title: SAT Math 800 Prep Course
program_overview.tagline: The Digital SAT rewards thinkers. We build the thinker, then train the test.
program_overview.description: A 6-month live prep program for the Digital SAT's math section, taught 1-on-1 or in small groups by a dedicated mentor. The SAT math section rewards structure recognition and clean algebra far more than memorized tricks, so we teach the mathematics underneath first, linear and nonlinear equations, functions, ratios, data, then layer on adaptive-test strategy: module pacing, Desmos calculator fluency, and when to solve versus when to backsolve. Every student runs full timed mock cycles with question-by-question review, so score movement is measured, not hoped for.
what_makes_this_different: Understanding-first: we repair the algebra underneath before drilling question types, because that is where 600-scorers become 750-scorers
Digital SAT native: adaptive module strategy and built-in Desmos calculator workflows taught as first-class skills
A dedicated mentor who tracks your error log across the whole program, not a rotating cast
Full mock cycles under real timing with question-by-question review against the reasoning, not just the answer key
One full interactive hour per class, twice a week, no 25-minute check-ins
Honest score guidance: a diagnostic in week one and evidence-based targets, never invented score promises
Small groups capped tight, or fully private 1-on-1 mentorship
Every class recorded for revision before test day

learning_path.phase_1: Diagnostic + the algebra core: linear equations, systems, inequalities and the function idea, repaired to fluency
learning_path.phase_2: Advanced Math and Problem-Solving & Data: quadratics, exponentials, ratios, percentages and data interpretation
learning_path.phase_3: Test craft: adaptive pacing, Desmos mastery, full mock cycles and error-log-driven polishing to peak on test day

career_outcomes: A math section score that opens selective-college doors, built on real understanding
Algebra and function fluency that carries directly into AP courses and college math
Calculator-smart, time-disciplined exam habits that transfer to every future standardized test
A documented mock-score progression parents and counselors can actually see

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: The Algebra Core (Weeks 1-8)
phase_1_foundation.description: The Digital SAT's biggest domain is algebra. This phase diagnoses exactly where points are leaking and rebuilds linear equations, systems, inequalities and functions to reconstruction strength, the foundation every later question type stands on.

week_1_2.title: Weeks 1-2: Diagnostic + Linear Equations Rebuilt
week_1_2.theme: Know the starting line, repair the base
topics: Full timed diagnostic module with question-by-question review
Personal error log opened: content gaps vs speed gaps vs careless patterns
Linear equations and expressions from meaning, not moves
Translating word problems into equations, the SAT's favorite disguise
Slope and intercepts as rates and starting values in context
Desmos introduction: graphing as a checking habit
projects: Personal score map: your exact domains, question types and target
First timed mini-module under real Digital SAT pacing
week_1_2.practice: Complete one timed linear equations mini set, log every miss in your error log as content, speed or careless, and redo the misses untimed.

week_3_4.title: Weeks 3-4: Systems and Inequalities
topics: Systems solved three ways: substitution, elimination, Desmos intersection
How many solutions? Parallel, intersecting and identical lines as ideas
Linear inequalities and shaded regions read from the graph
Systems word problems: mixtures, rates, break-even setups
When solving is slower than testing answer choices, strategy honestly taught
Timed reps: the 60-second system question
projects: Break-even mini-case solved by equation, table and graph
Error-log review: your system-question mistakes classified and closed
week_3_4.practice: Work a 15 question systems and inequalities set, solving at least five by Desmos intersection, and file each error in your log with its fix.

week_5_6.title: Weeks 5-6: Functions as Machines
week_5_6.theme: The idea the hard modules assume
topics: Function notation read as a sentence: f(x) is an output, not a mystery
Evaluating, composing and interpreting functions in context
Graph reading: intercepts, increasing/decreasing, transformations
Linear function word problems and modeling
Function questions in the calculator module: Desmos-first workflows
Timed reps: function questions across both modules
projects: Function-transformation exploration built live in Desmos
Mixed timed set: algebra domain at full pace
week_5_6.practice: Finish a mixed function notation problem set, then rebuild two missed questions in Desmos to see what the graph was telling you.

week_7_8.title: Weeks 7-8: Algebra Domain Checkpoint
topics: Full algebra-domain timed section under adaptive conditions
Review ritual taught: why each miss happened, what changes next time
Absolute value and compound inequalities, the fringe cases
Careless-error engineering: writing habits that stop point leaks
Module-1 pacing plan drafted against your speed data
projects: Algebra checkpoint mock + personal pacing plan v1
Updated error log with closed vs open gap list
week_7_8.practice: Take one timed algebra domain section and write a one line cause and correction for every dropped point before the review class.

phase_2_advanced_math.title: PHASE 2: Advanced Math + Problem-Solving & Data (Weeks 9-18)
phase_2_advanced_math.description: The domains that separate good scores from great ones: quadratics and exponentials taught as ideas, then the ratio-percent-data cluster where careless points go to die. Every topic is drilled at real SAT difficulty tiers, including the hard-module versions.

week_9_11.title: Weeks 9-11: Quadratics and Nonlinear Equations
topics: Quadratics four ways: factoring, completing the square, formula, Desmos
The discriminant as an idea: how many solutions and why the SAT asks
Vertex form and parabola word problems (projectiles, revenue, area)
Nonlinear systems: line meets parabola, read from the graph
Exponent rules rebuilt from meaning; rational exponents and radicals
Hard-module quadratic questions: recognizing the structure in costume
projects: Projectile/revenue modeling mini-case solved by vertex reasoning
Timed hard-module quadratic set with full review
week_9_11.practice: Drill a 12 question hard module quadratic set, solving each question twice: once by algebra and once by the fastest Desmos route.

week_12_14.title: Weeks 12-14: Exponential Growth, Polynomials and Rational Expressions
topics: Linear vs exponential growth: the comparison the SAT loves to test
Exponential word problems: percent growth/decay, doubling, interest
Polynomial arithmetic, zeros and graph behavior
Rational expressions and equations without fear
Isolating variables in formulas, the classic layup question
Equivalent-expression questions: the algebra-fluency tax
projects: Growth-model comparison case: same data, linear vs exponential fits
Advanced Math domain checkpoint under timing
week_12_14.practice: Complete a percent growth and decay problem set plus five isolate the variable questions, keeping each layup question under a minute.

week_15_18.title: Weeks 15-18: Problem-Solving & Data Analysis
week_15_18.theme: Ratios, percents and data, the careless-point graveyard
topics: Ratio and proportion reasoning: the choose-the-easy-comparison skill
Percent problems in every SAT costume: change, reverse, repeated
Unit conversion and rates with dimensional discipline
Reading tables, scatterplots, histograms and box plots fast
Line of best fit, slope interpretation and prediction questions
Mean, median, range and standard deviation as concepts
Margin of error and study-design questions, what the SAT actually asks
Two-way tables and conditional probability without formulas
projects: Data-interpretation sprint: 20 real chart questions under time
Full mixed-domain mock: first complete Digital SAT math section
week_15_18.practice: Run a 20 question ratio, percent and data interpretation sprint under time and classify every miss before class.

phase_3_test_mastery.title: PHASE 3: Adaptive Test Craft + Mock Cycles (Weeks 19-26)
phase_3_test_mastery.description: Content becomes score in this phase: adaptive module strategy, Desmos speed workflows, geometry-and-trig completion, and a full mock cycle rhythm, test, review, repair, retest, that peaks the week of the real exam.

week_19_20.title: Weeks 19-20: Geometry and Trigonometry Domain
topics: Area, volume and the reference-sheet formulas used intelligently
Triangles: similarity, right-triangle trig, special right triangles
Circles: arcs, sectors, and the circle equation
Angle chasing with parallel lines and polygons
Coordinate geometry: distance, midpoint, and geometric reasoning on the grid
Trig on the SAT: exactly what is tested, nothing more
projects: Geometry domain checkpoint under timing
Personal formula-and-recognition sheet, built not copied
week_19_20.practice: Finish a geometry domain set using your own recognition sheet wherever possible, starring any formula you still had to look up.

week_21_23.title: Weeks 21-23: Adaptive Strategy and Desmos Mastery
topics: How Digital SAT adaptivity works and what module 1 accuracy is worth
Pacing plans per module built from your own timing data
Desmos power workflows: solving, systems, regressions, tables at speed
Solve vs backsolve vs plug-in: choosing the fastest honest route
Flag-and-return discipline; the two-pass module method
Guessing strategy and the no-blank rule, applied calmly
projects: Desmos speed drills: 15 questions where the calculator wins
Strategy card: your personal decision rules for module 2
week_21_23.practice: Do the 15 question Desmos speed drill, record your per question times and flag anything over 90 seconds for strategy review.

week_24_26.title: Weeks 24-26: Full Mock Cycles and Peak Week
week_24_26.theme: Test day as a rehearsed place
topics: Full adaptive mocks under exact timing, reviewed question by question
Error-log closure: every recurring mistake gets a named fix
Score-trend review with honest final-target calibration
Peak-week plan: what to practice, what to rest, sleep and logistics
Test-day routine: warm-up set, timing checkpoints, reset ritual after hard questions
projects: Two to three full mock cycles with documented score progression
Written test-day plan, rehearsed twice before the real thing
week_24_26.practice: Sit one full adaptive mock under exact timing this week and bring the question by question review with your named fixes.

faqs.question: Is this course for the Digital SAT?
faqs.answer: Yes, the course is built for the Digital SAT format: two adaptive math modules, the built-in Desmos graphing calculator, and current question styles. Module strategy and Desmos fluency are taught as first-class skills alongside the mathematics, and we verify current test details against College Board materials for your test date.

faqs.question: What score improvement can we expect?
faqs.answer: We will not invent a number, improvement depends on the starting score, weeks available and practice done between classes. What we promise instead: a real diagnostic in week one, a plan that attacks your highest-value gaps first, and a documented mock-score progression you can watch. Students who complete the mock cycles see their trend line clearly before test day.

faqs.question: My teen scores well in school math but low on SAT practice tests. Why?
faqs.answer: This is the most common profile we see. School rewards showing your work over weeks; the SAT rewards structure recognition under a clock. The gap is usually speed, question-reading and strategy rather than missing content, all trainable. The diagnostic separates content gaps from craft gaps in the first week.

faqs.question: When should SAT math prep start?
faqs.answer: The full program is 6 months, which comfortably fits a spring test from an autumn start. Starting closer to the test still works, the mentor compresses toward your highest-value domains and mock cycles, but more runway means the algebra core gets properly rebuilt rather than patched.

faqs.question: How is this different from a prep-book or app-based course?
faqs.answer: Books and apps give practice; they cannot watch your teen think. A live mentor catches the broken step inside the work, the sign error, the misread, the slow route, and fixes the habit, not just the answer. That is why our classes are a full interactive hour with your teen solving and explaining aloud, twice a week.

faqs.question: Do you cover the reading and writing section too?
faqs.answer: This course is deliberately math-only, depth over bundling. Math is where a specialist mentor moves scores fastest, and where our maths-and-coding teaching method gives a real edge. Families wanting verbal prep can run it in parallel elsewhere without conflict.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1. Both include 8 live one-hour classes a month, recordings, and the free demo class before any payment.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every student starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the diagnostic, a real lesson plus an honest read on the starting point and target, with no card details required.

testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

prerequisites.time_commitment: 2 live hours plus 2-3 practice hours weekly
prerequisites.motivation: A target test date, or our help choosing one

who_is_this_for.score_raisers: Students scoring 500-650 on practice tests who need the algebra core rebuilt, not more drills
who_is_this_for.top_end: Students at 700+ hunting the last 60-100 points through hard-module craft and error elimination
who_is_this_for.strong_at_school: Teens with good school grades whose practice scores do not match, the classic craft gap
who_is_this_for.planners: Grade 10 families starting early so the real test year is calm
who_is_this_for.retakers: Students whose first attempt underperformed and who want a method, not more of the same

Ready for AP Calculus Exam Prep or the College Mathematics Masterclass

course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.
